lines.text: Next is Ground Force, the physical moat that no software-only competitor can copy.
Anyone can build a website, but only Nguzo puts real people next to the machines and at the borders.
On the left, every field inspection your inspectors have carried out, with their notes and sign-off status.
Each entry is a real person who stood next to a machine and confirmed it exists and actually works.
The status pill tells you whether each inspection is still pending or fully verified.
On the right, the border logs, recorded by your liaison agents stationed at the crossings.
They capture the institutional wait time at each post, and it turns amber when a crossing is stalling.
This is how Nguzo turns up at the border in person, instead of leaving a supplier stranded at a portal failure.
Boots on the ground is the moat, and this desk is where you watch it work, shift after shift.
